00027  * SUCH DAMAGE.

00029 
00030 /*
00031  * Create a large file in small increments.
00032  *
00033  * Should work on emufs (emu0:) once the basic system calls are done,
00034  * and should work on SFS when the file system assignment is
00035  * done. Sufficiently small files should work on SFS even before that
00036  * assignment.
00037  */
00038 
00039 #include <stdlib.h>
00040 #include <stdio.h>
00041 #include <string.h>
00042 #include <unistd.h>
00043 #include <err.h>
00044 
00045 static char buffer[100];
00046 
00047 int
00048 main(int argc, char *argv[])
00049 {
00050         const char *filename;
00051         int i, size;
00052         int fileid;
00053         int len;
00054 
00055         if (argc != 3) {
00056                 errx(1, "Usage: bigfile <filename> <size>");
00057         }
00058 
00059         filename = argv[1];
00060         size = atoi(argv[2]);
00061 
00062         printf("Creating a file of size %d\n", size);
00063 
00064         fileid = open(filename, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC);
00065         if (fileid < 0) {
00066                 err(1, "%s: create", filename);
00067         }
00068 
00069         i=0;
00070         while (i<size) {
00071                 snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%-10d", i);
00072                 len = write(fileid, buffer, strlen(buffer));
00073                 if (len<0) {
00074                         err(1, "%s: write", filename);
00075                 }
00076                 i += len;
00077         }       
00078 
00079         close(fileid);
00080 
00081         return 0;
00082 }
